start_urls.title: 🔗 Indeed Search URLs
start_urls.description: Paste one or more Indeed job search URLs. To get these: go to Indeed → search for a job → copy the URL from your browser. You can add multiple URLs (different roles, locations, etc.).

about_me.title: 🎯 Your Target Job Titles
about_me.description: Enter the job titles you are looking for, one per line. The AI uses these to score how well each job matches you — only jobs above your minimum score will be saved. Be specific for better results.
about_me.prefill:
Software Engineer
Backend Developer
Python Developer
Django Developer

max_jobs.title: 📦 How Many Jobs to Collect?
max_jobs.description: The scraper stops once it has collected this many matching jobs. Set higher if you want more results. Recommended: 50–200.

per_company_jobs.title: 🏢 Max Jobs Per Company
per_company_jobs.description: Limits how many jobs from the same company are included. For example, setting this to 3 means you won't get more than 3 jobs from Google, Amazon, etc. Helps keep results diverse.

min_match_percentage.title: 🤖 Minimum AI Match Score (0–100)
min_match_percentage.description: The AI scores each job title against your target titles. Only jobs that score at or above this number are saved. Lower = more results but less relevant. Higher = fewer but more targeted. Recommended: 30–50.

ignore_companies.title: 🚫 Companies to Skip (optional)
ignore_companies.description:
Any jobs from these companies will be ignored. Enter one company name per line. Useful for filtering out staffing agencies or companies you've already applied to.

Example:
Google
Amazon
Staffing Solutions Inc

ignore_related.title: 🚫 Keywords to Skip (optional)
ignore_related.description:
If a job description contains any of these words or phrases, that job will be skipped. Enter one keyword per line. Useful for filtering out roles that don't fit (e.g. security clearance requirements, specific tech stacks you want to avoid).

Example:
clearance required
C++
management consulting

google_sheet_url.title: 📊 Google Sheet URL (optional)
google_sheet_url.description: If you want results exported to Google Sheets, paste the full URL of your sheet here. Make sure the sheet is shared with your service account email. Leave blank to skip — results are always saved in the Apify dataset regardless.

sheet_name.title: 📋 Google Sheet Tab Name
sheet_name.description: The name of the tab (worksheet) inside your Google Sheet where results will be written. If the tab doesn't exist, it will be created automatically.
